title: Custom tool manifest
description: Registers a tool with Atelier's built-in tool registry (§15). Built-in tools (read_file, write_file, shell, grep, ...) are shipped in atelier-core; custom tools are added by dropping a manifest file in `~/.atelier/tools/` (global) or `<repo>/.atelier/tools/` (per-repo, overrides global by name). The tool is exposed internally through the same MCP-compatible interface as external MCP-routed tools; the rest of the harness (verification gates, hooks, ledger, trust budget) treats built-in, custom, and MCP-routed tools uniformly.

name.description: Tool identifier surfaced to the model and in the §1 cost ledger. Must be unique within the session; per-repo manifests override same-name global ones.

description.description: Surfaced to the model as the tool's natural-language description. Should explain what the tool does, when to use it, and what arguments it takes.

side_effect_class.description: §8 trust-budget classification. Overridable per-call via the trust-budget UI, but this is the default.

input_schema.description: JSONSchema for the tool's argument object. The harness validates the model's tool-call arguments against this before dispatch; SchemaViolation (§2.5) on mismatch.

output_schema.description: Optional JSONSchema for the tool's result object. If present, the harness validates the tool's result before injecting it into the conversation; SchemaViolation (§2.5) on mismatch.

allow_net.description: Shell-only: if true, the subprocess sandbox permits outbound network. HTTP implementation implies network and ignores this flag.

deadline_ms.description: v60.29 H9 — per-tool wall-clock deadline. The dispatcher races the tool future against this duration; on expiry the call returns ToolError::Deadline (§2.5 Retry). Omit to inherit the runner default (60_000ms / 60s).
